name: Las Vencías Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse de las Vencías y pueblo de Fuentidueña — circular desde San Miguel de Bernuy
description: Hike a difficult 12.7-mile circular route from San Miguel de Bernuy, exploring the Duratón River, Embalse de las Vencías, and Fuentidueña.
region_intro: Hiking around San Miguel De Bernuy offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park. The region is characterized by deep river gorges carved by the Duratón River, serene reservoir waters, and a mix of high plateau and riverside vegetation. Trails often lead through areas with ash trees, willows, and oak groves, alongside impressive vertical cliffs. This varied terrain provides diverse hiking experiences for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around San Miguel De Bernuy The most popular hiking route is Las Vencías Reservoir Loop, a 12.7 miles (20.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Duratón River, the Embalse de las Vencías, and extends towards Fuentidueña, showcasing dramatic canyon landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Monasterio de la Hoz – Convento de la Hoz Viewpoint loop from Burgomillodo, a moderate 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This trail offers panoramic vistas of the Duratón River's meanders and the Hoz Monastery, accessible via a forest track. Local hikers also love the Ermita de San Frutos – Hermitage of San Frutos loop from Aparcamiento Ermita de San Frutos, a 1.5 miles (2.4 km) trail leading through the natural setting around the significant Hermitage of San Frutos, often completed in about 48 minutes. Hiking around San Miguel De Bernuy is defined by spectacular river gorges, the wildlife-rich Embalse de las Vencías, and historical ruins. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in San Miguel De Bernuy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 80 hikers have used komoot to explore San Miguel De Bernuy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: Hiking around San Miguel de Bernuy offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter dramatic river gorges carved by the Duratón River, serene reservoir waters, and a mix of high plateau (paramera) and riverside vegetation. Trails often lead through areas with ash trees, willows, and oak groves, alongside impressive vertical cliffs. The area is part of the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park, known for its rugged beauty and varied ecosystems.

Q: Are there hiking trails for all skill levels in San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: Yes, San Miguel de Bernuy offers a variety of hiking trails suitable for different fitness levels. You can find easy walks, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 1.5 miles (2.4 km), as well as more challenging routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156747003/las-vencias-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Vencías Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 12.7 miles (20.5 km).

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking near San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the spectacular Duratón River Gorges, the tranquil Embalse de las Vencías reservoir, and various viewpoints. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6204193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos&lt;/a&gt;, known for its stunning natural setting, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5791277" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of vulture nests&lt;/a&gt;, where you can observe raptors. Many trails also pass by ruins of ancient hermitages and medieval structures.

Q: Where can I find the best panoramic views of the Duratón River?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views of the Duratón River's meanders and the Hoz Monastery, consider hiking the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19811047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Monasterio de la Hoz – Convento de la Hoz Viewpoint loop from Burgomillodo&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 7.6-mile (12.2 km) path is accessible via a beautiful forest track and offers excellent vistas.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: Yes, there are family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos – Hermitage of San Frutos loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 1.5-mile (2.4 km) trail that is suitable for families. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of vulture nests loop from San Miguel de Bernuy&lt;/a&gt; is another easy option, covering 3.4 miles (5.5 km), offering opportunities to spot wildlife.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in wildlife, especially birds. Along the cliffs, you can spot various raptors such as griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, peregrine falcons, and kestrels. Near the river, if you are quiet, you might see otters, American minks, grey herons, and ducks. The paramera is home to rabbits, hares, and foxes, while partridges and common larks are common birds.

Q: Are the hiking trails around San Miguel de Bernuy dog-friendly?
A: Many trails in natural areas like the Hoces del Río Duratón Natural Park are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect the diverse wildlife, especially ground animals like rabbits and hares, and nesting birds. Always ensure your dog is under control and respect local regulations, particularly in protected areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: The hiking routes in San Miguel de Bernuy are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Hikers often praise the spectacular river gorges, the wildlife-rich Embalse de las Vencías, and the historical ruins scattered throughout the captivating landscape. The variety of routes catering to different abilities is also a highlight.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1156747003/las-vencias-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Vencías Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 12.7-mile (20.5 km) circular route. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19811047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Monasterio de la Hoz – Convento de la Hoz Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.6 miles (12.2 km) long. For an easier option, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31506585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 1.5-mile (2.4 km) circular trail.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in San Miguel de Bernuy?
A: The region offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Spring brings blooming flora, while autumn showcases beautiful foliage. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended. Winter hiking is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially some wet conditions, especially along the river.

Q: Are there any historical ruins or ancient sites to discover on the trails?
A: Absolutely. The hiking trails around San Miguel de Bernuy often lead past fascinating historical ruins. You can discover ancient hermitages like 'Las Ermitonas,' 'Los Sanpedros,' and 'Los Sanmartines,' as well as medieval castles and villas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6204193" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Frutos&lt;/a&gt; and the nearby ruins of the Hermitage of San Valentín are also significant historical sites in the broader Duratón area.
tips: The Ruins of Los Sampedros in San Miguel de Bernuy are an interesting archaeological site. Although there are no specific historical citations, it is believed that it had a Celtiberian occupation and was then abandoned in Roman times. 😊

Fuentidueña has remains of Romanesque walls, several gates and towers. Among them, the Puerta de Alfonso VIII stands out, part of the remains of the castle that today is in ruins. The castle, located in the upper part of a large walled area, had the function of a fortress and served as a last stronghold in case of attack on the population. 😊🏰

Between the town of San Miguel de Bernuy and the Villa de Fuentidueña, the Duratón River has excavated a deep canyon that is filled with the waters of the Las Vencias reservoir. The spectacular views of the cuts that the river has traced in its path are mixed with remains of an old fortress, a hermitage and a convent. There are vultures and we can see griffon vultures in their majestic flight. Among the activities that this attractive place offers us is sailing in a canoe or canoe from San Miguel de Bernuy to the end of the reservoir, enjoying the silence and delighting in the observation of nature.🌞😎

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Embalse de las Vencías and pueblo de Fuentidueña circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers flexibility, allowing you to start from either San Miguel de Bernuy or Fuentidueña. Parking is generally available in or near these villages.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features varied terrain. You'll encounter paths crossing the Duratón River, rocky sections, ascents to a plateau, and stretches along high plains with cereal fields. Near Fuentidueña, the path winds through vineyards and almond groves. Be prepared for some stony sections, especially on access roads to the reservoir.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience a fantastic natural landscape with spectacular views, especially along the Duratón River's gorges. Notable highlights include the dramatic transition of the river from its deep canyon to the more open 'La Serranilla' area, and views of vulture nests on the high cliffs.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while hiking?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. The high cliffs are nesting grounds for various raptors, including griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, and red-billed choughs. You might also spot rabbits, hares, partridges, foxes, otters, American mink, grey herons, and mallards in different parts of the ecosystem.

faqs.question: What historical sites or landmarks are along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in monumental heritage. You can explore the Ruins of San Miguel, remnants of Romanesque hermitages like 'Las Ermitonas', and the medieval Castle and village of Fuentidueña.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, dogs are generally welcome. However, as it is located within the Hoces del Río Duratón, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and nesting birds.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers different experiences throughout the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During warmer months, the Embalse de las Vencías offers opportunities for swimming and kayaking, providing a refreshing break.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail, given it's in Hoces del Río Duratón?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to hike this specific trail within the Hoces del Río Duratón natural park. However, always respect local regulations regarding wildlife and environmental protection.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse de las Vencías and pueblo de Fuentidueña circular trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is generally classified as having a "Medium" to "Medium-High" difficulty. While not overly strenuous, it involves varied terrain and some ascents/descents, making it more suitable for hikers with some experience rather than absolute beginners. Active families might enjoy it, but be prepared for a longer hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Calle Callerío and the Camino al Embalse de las Vencias.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants in San Miguel de Bernuy or Fuentidueña?
faqs.answer: Both San Miguel de Bernuy and Fuentidueña are small villages that may offer limited amenities such as cafes or restaurants, especially during peak season. It's advisable to check opening hours in advance or bring your own provisions.
